title: How Southeast Asia’s Tourism Sector Changed in 2025
gist: Southeast Asia's 2025 tourism recovered to 2018 levels (~119M visitors across six countries) but stayed below the 2019 peak of ~127M, with Thailand's long-standing regional dominance beginning to erode as arrivals fell 7 percent.

insights.claim: Just under 119 million foreign visitors arrived in Singapore, Malaysia, Vietnam, Indonesia, Thailand, and the Philippines in 2025, back to 2018 levels but below the 2019 high of ~127 million.
insights.speaker: James Guild
insights.so_what: The region has not fully recovered pre-pandemic tourism, capping the recovery trade in SEA travel-exposed names.
insights.locator: In 2025, just under 119 million foreign visitors

insights.claim: Thailand recorded almost 33 million inbound arrivals in 2025, still the region's largest by a wide margin, but arrivals fell 7 percent versus 2024 and remain well below the 2019 peak of nearly 40 million.
insights.speaker: James Guild
insights.so_what: Thailand's declining share is the key SEA tourism shift to watch for rotation toward Vietnam and others.

insights.claim: Beneath the headline numbers, important shifts are underway and Thailand's long-standing status as Southeast Asia's largest tourism draw is starting to change.
insights.speaker: James Guild
insights.so_what: Signals a competitive reshuffle in SEA tourism worth tracking for country-level exposure.
insights.locator: Whenever you talk about tourism in Southeast Asia
